negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Block verification rejects malformed delivery-result extraction and short SPC proofs, but no local verifier path rejects a transaction merely because it carries more than one SPC call; the current code selects a matching call with any/find.
related_but_insufficient_code: verify_spc_calls returns InvalidSpc for short proofs and missing matching calls.
verify_settlements extracts delivery_result from calldata and maps classification/extraction errors to InvalidAsyncSettlement.
validate_async_settlement_shape checks that at least one delivery SPC call exists but does not reject extra calls.

notes: Confirmed gap: several malformed paths are fail-closed, but the more-than-one-SPC-call case is not enforced.
rationale: The primary diff replaces delivery_result_len.unwrap_or(0) with an error and changes SPC extraction from warn-and-skip behavior to Result-returning hard rejection for malformed async SPC data. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Block verification should reject blocks when Phase 2 delivery_result_len cannot be extracted, a transaction contains more than one SPC call, an async SPC proof is too short, or the SPC executor address cannot be extracted or is zero.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create block-verification fixtures with a Phase 2 settlement whose input cannot yield delivery_result_len, a transaction with two SPC calls, a proof shorter than SPC_PROOF_SIGNATURE_LENGTH, and an SPC call with zero or unextractable executor address. Each should make verification return an error instead of passing after skipping/defaulting.
summary: Malformed SPC checks lack the multi-SPC rejection required by the invariant.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create a settlement/original transaction with two SPC calls where one matches; the invariant expects rejection, while current verification can select the matching call and continue.
title: Malformed SPC and Phase 2 settlement data rejects blocks
